name: INTELLECT-3
archDisplay: MoE, post-trained via SFT + large-scale asynchronous RL on top of Zhipu's open GLM-4.5-Air-Base
note: Prime Intellect (decentralized/open RL training startup) post-trained this 106B/12B-active MoE on top of the openly-licensed GLM-4.5-Air-Base checkpoint, then open-sourced not just the weights but the full RL stack (PRIME-RL framework, verifiers library, 500+ agentic RL environments) under permissive licenses. Served via Prime Intellect's own chat.primeintellect.ai and Inference API; hosted pricing not publicly confirmed.

results.notes: GPQA Diamond [Corrected during full-corpus audit: The INTELLECT-3 Technical Report's own Table 2 ('Evaluations', Section 4) explicitly lists INTELLECT-3's GPQA Diamond score as 74.4, not 76.1. The existing 76.1 figure was sourced from Prime Intellect's blog post, whose only benchmark presentation is a bar chart (Figure 1 in the same report) without printed axis values — the precise, explicitly labeled Table 2 in the technical report is the more a]
Humanity's Last Exam, Table 2 of the INTELLECT-3 Technical Report.
Table 2 of the INTELLECT-3 Technical Report.

MATH-500. Reported in the official Prime Intellect HF model card evaluation table alongside AIME24/AIME25/LCB/GPQA/HLE/MMLU-Pro (same 6 metrics as the technical report's Table 2, plus this one extra column). Not present in the arXiv technical report itself. INTELLECT-3 scores 98.1 vs. GLM-4.5-Air's 97.8 and GLM-4.5's 97.0 on the same vendor table.

verificationNotes:
Cross-checked the INTELLECT-3 technical report (arXiv:2512.16144) against Prime Intellect's own blog post and the Hugging Face repo (PrimeIntellect/INTELLECT-3); benchmark numbers are vendor-reported in both the paper and blog.

[2026-07-16 gap-fill pass, mmlu/humaneval/swe-bench/mmmu] All four core-benchmark gaps resolved as omitted_no_evidence after checking official materials + independent leaderboards (2+ source types each, per rule): (1) MMLU (plain) — Prime Intellect's blog, arXiv technical report (Table 2, both PDF and HTML mirrors), and HF model card all report only MMLU-Pro (81.9), never plain MMLU; INTELLECT-3 does not appear on llm-stats.com's MMLU leaderboard (100 models). The base model GLM-4.5-Air does report plain MMLU (87.4) in its own paper, confirming Prime Intellect deliberately did not carry that metric forward — consistent with the field's general shift to MMLU-Pro as MMLU's successor. (2) HumanEval — absent from all official INTELLECT-3 materials (blog/paper/HF card report LiveCodeBench v6 instead as the modern coding eval) and absent from llm-stats.com's HumanEval leaderboard (66 models). Legacy/saturated benchmark, expected omission. (3) SWE-bench (any variant) — absent from Table 2 of the technical report, the blog, and the HF card; INTELLECT-3 does not appear on llm-stats.com's SWE-bench Verified leaderboard (104 models). Notably, Prime Intellect's own docs (docs.primeintellect.ai/tutorials-environments/environments) describe SWE-bench-derived datasets (R2E-Gym, SWE-smith, Multi-SWE-bench) as RL *training* environments used to build INTELLECT-3, which plausibly explains why they did not also report it as a held-out eval (train/test contamination risk) rather than a simple oversight. (4) MMMU — structurally inapplicable: INTELLECT-3 is confirmed text-only (modalities: ["text"], capabilities.vision: false in this corpus entry, and the arXiv report contains no mention of vision/multimodal capability), and MMMU is a vision/multimodal benchmark. No changes made to any existing result.

[2026-07-18 targeted re-chase, swe-bench/mmmu] Re-opened both gaps per instruction to search harder before accepting absence, since SWE-bench Verified and MMMU are current-generation benchmarks most active vendors still report. Result: prior 2026-07-16 omitted_no_evidence conclusions for both stand, now backed by a substantially deeper source sweep (methods 1, 2, 3, and 5 of the 6 listed, i.e. official materials, independent trackers, academic/GitHub, and sibling-model comparison tables).

SWE-bench Verified — checked and found ABSENT in all of: (a) official launch blog (primeintellect.ai/blog/intellect-3 — no benchmark table, only an unlabeled training-progress chart); (b) official HF model card evaluation table (PrimeIntellect/INTELLECT-3 — 7 columns: MATH-500/AIME24/AIME25/LCB/GPQA/HLE/MMLU-Pro, no SWE-bench); (c) the arXiv technical report (2512.16144), full-text-searched across both the PDF and HTML mirrors for every occurrence of "SWE-bench", "SWE bench", and "R2E-Gym" — the only hits are in Section 3.1.6 describing R2E-Gym/SWE-smith/Multi-SWE-bench as RL *training environment* formats, never as a held-out eval with a reported score for INTELLECT-3; (d) independent trackers Artificial Analysis (artificialanalysis.ai/models/intellect-3 — reports GDPval-AA, tau3-Banking, Terminal-Bench v2.1, SciCode, HLE, GPQA Diamond, CritPt, AA-Omniscience, AA-LCR, but no SWE-bench) and Benchable (benchable.ai — reports Math/Coding/Knowledge/Ethics/Reasoning composite percentiles, no SWE-bench); (e) swebench.com's own leaderboard (searched directly — no INTELLECT-3 entry); (f) llm-stats.com (no page/mention for INTELLECT-3 at all as of this pass); (g) Epoch AI's benchmark directory (searched, no INTELLECT-3 results surfaced); (h) OpenRouter's model page (no benchmark table); (i) third-party launch-day coverage (implicator.ai) which reproduces the same 5-metric set as the blog (AIME24/25, LCB, GPQA, MMLU-Pro) with no SWE-bench. No sibling-model launch table found citing an INTELLECT-3 SWE-bench score either. Conclusion: still not found after an exhaustive pass — proposing omitted_no_evidence again, unchanged score data.

MMMU — reconfirmed architecturally inapplicable: this corpus entry's own modalities field is ["text"] and capabilities.vision is false; the arXiv technical report contains no mention of vision/multimodal capability anywhere in its full text; and an independent web search explicitly corroborates "INTELLECT-3 is a text-only model and does not support multimodal vision capabilities." MMMU (and MMMU-Pro) are vision-reasoning benchmarks requiring image inputs, which this model cannot accept. Proposing omitted_no_evidence with reason "architecturally inapplicable (text-only model)" — this is a modality mismatch, not a reporting gap, so no further searching would change the answer.

Both benchmark slugs (swe-bench, mmmu) confirmed to exist in corpus/benchmarks.json, so the gaps were real slugs to check, not typos. No changes made to any existing result row.
